data.narrative:
Transmutex is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Franklin Servan-Schreiber, Federico Carminati, and Jean-Pierre Revol, having raised $23M to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power, though the narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier remains limited.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $23M total raised proxy, implying a $46M valuation. This reflects typical capital step-ups between Series A and subsequent funding rounds. The peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient data density—while 8 nuclear peers exist sector-wide, only 1-2 met narrow filtering criteria, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints needed for reliable median anchoring. Broader peer-set medians risk undue noise given the early, heterogeneous nature of frontier nuclear ventures.

The triangulation yields a median of $46M with a range of $39M–$53M, derived from the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for pre-revenue frontier tech (2.0x may understate risk or overstate sector momentum), technology maturity and regulatory pathway clarity, and the degree to which downstream public comparables reflect private frontier-stage risk. Further validation would require deeper nuclear peer financials or market intelligence on recent Series A rounds in the sector.
